extra.reasoning:
The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on all reachable executions.

Key observations from the transition relation:
1. sent := true is set ONLY by t_send_allowed, which is guarded by `not opted_out`. Therefore at the exact moment sent becomes true, opted_out is necessarily false.
2. opted_out := true is set ONLY by t_optout, which in the same atomic update also sets sent := false. So the state entered when opted_out flips to true always has sent = false.
3. opted_out is monotonic: no transition ever sets opted_out := false. Once true, it remains true on all subsequent steps.
4. While opted_out is true, no transition can make sent true: t_send_allowed is disabled (guard not opted_out is false); t_send_blocked sets sent := false; t_tick sets sent := false; t_optout sets sent := false. Every other event with no matching enabled transition leaves state unchanged.

Therefore, in every reachable state, if opted_out is true then sent is false. The initial state (opted_out=false) satisfies the implication vacuously, and the inductive invariant (opted_out => not sent) is preserved by every event. The property holds within the 10-event bound and in fact for all executions.
